content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

All four amendments identify real correctness or recovery defects in the bead export path. The current implementation emits hierarchy edges with the wrong dependency kind, does not provide a recoverable retry identity across CLI invocations, skips required comments on reused beads after partial failures, and can make idempotent reuse ambiguous when a workstream title matches the milestone title.

## Votes

- `fr-2-44f5298d`: Accept
  `materialize_beads()` sends both hierarchy links and ordinary proposal dependencies through the same `ensure_dependency()` helper, and that helper always calls `BrMutationAdapter::add_dependency()` with no dependency kind. `BrCommand::dep_add()` therefore builds `br dep add <from> <to>` without `--type`, and the local `br dep add --help` shows the default is `blocks`. Downstream code such as `infer_parent_epic_id()` only recognizes `DependencyKind::ParentChild`, so the exported milestone/workstream/task hierarchy is materially wrong.
- `fr-2-02669566`: Accept
  `handle_export_beads()` constructs `BrMutationAdapter::with_adapter(...)`, which gives each invocation a fresh random adapter id. After any successful mutation, `exec_tracked_mutation_locked()` persists a pending-mutation record; if a later step fails, that record remains. The next run hits `ensure_bead_export_preflight()` -> `ensure_synced()`, which rejects any pending local mutations before export can continue, and the new adapter id means there is no ownership-based replay path comparable to `sync_own_dirty_if_beads_healthy()`. That makes automatic retry after a partial export failure impossible without manual intervention.
- `fr-2-28ce7798`: Accept
  The code only adds the workstream description comment when `workstream_epic.created` is true and only adds the planning-rationale comment when `bead.created` is true. On a rerun, reused beads do not check whether those comments are already present, so a failure after bead creation but before comment creation leaves the export permanently incomplete while still allowing a later run to report success. This is a real idempotency gap in the export contract, even though the bead objects themselves may be reused correctly.
- `fr-2-e87ed6c1`: Accept
  Bundle validation does not reject a workstream name equal to `identity.name`, so a milestone root epic and a workstream epic can legitimately share the same title. Reuse matching is based on title/type plus 'requested labels are a subset of existing labels', which lets the root epic also satisfy the workstream matcher on labels, and `find_existing_materialized_bead()` then errors when more than one milestone-scoped bead exists for that title. That breaks idempotent reruns for a valid input shape and needs either disjoint identity criteria or earlier validation.
